hook: Want to visit the *closest* star to our Sun?
facts: Proxima Centauri is the nearest star to us, beyond the Sun.
It is 4.2 light-years away, which is roughly 40 TRILLION kilometers (25 trillion miles).
Our fastest spacecraft, Voyager 1, travels at an incredible ~61,000 km/h (~38,000 mph).
payoff: At Voyager 1's speed, it would take OVER 70,000 YEARS to reach Proxima Centauri. Hope you packed snacks!
title: Travel to the Closest Star? 🤯

hook: How far is the nearest star, *REALLY*? This scale will blow your mind!
facts: If our Sun was shrunk to the size of a grapefruit (10 cm diameter)...
...Earth would be a tiny grain of sand (0.1 cm) orbiting 10 meters away.
And Proxima Centauri, our closest star, on this exact scale?
payoff: It would be more than 2,800 KILOMETERS away! That's like the distance from London to Athens. Space is unimaginably vast.
title: 🤯🤯🤯 The Nearest Star is HOW FAR?! #SpaceScale

hook: How long would it take to reach the nearest star?
facts: The nearest star to our Sun is Proxima Centauri, 4.24 light-years (or ~40 trillion km) away.
The fastest human-made object ever recorded is NASA's Parker Solar Probe, which can reach speeds of 690,000 km/h (430,000 mph).
At that record-breaking speed, how long would it take to reach Proxima Centauri?
payoff: It would still take over 6,600 years to get there! Our cosmic neighborhood is truly vast.
title: Journey to the Nearest Star: How Long?! 🚀🌌
